params.name: table
params.description: Name of the database table or view

params.name: select
params.description: Comma-separated list of columns to return. Supports renaming (alias:column), casting (column::type), and embedding related resources via foreign keys (related_table(columns)).

params.name: order
params.description: Comma-separated list of columns to sort by. Append .desc for descending, .asc for ascending. Use .nullsfirst or .nullslast to control null ordering.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of rows to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of rows to skip before starting to return rows

items.docs: Retrieves rows from a database table with support for filtering, sorting, pagination, and column selection. PostgREST operators are used in query parameters for horizontal filtering (e.g. eq, neq, gt, lt, gte, lte, like, ilike, in, is, fts, plfts, phfts, wfts, cs, cd, ov, sl, sr, nxl, nxr, adj, not, or, and). Vertical filtering uses the select parameter.

params.name: table
params.description: Name of the database table or view

items.docs: Inserts one or more rows into a database table. Use the Prefer header with return=representation to get the inserted rows in the response. Supports upsert with Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates.

params.name: table
params.description: Name of the database table or view

items.docs: Updates rows in a database table that match the specified filters. All PostgREST filter operators can be used as query parameters to select which rows to update.

params.name: table
params.description: Name of the database table or view

items.docs: Deletes rows from a database table that match the specified filters. All PostgREST filter operators can be used as query parameters. It is strongly recommended to always include filters to avoid deleting all rows.

params.name: function_name
params.description: Name of the PostgreSQL function to invoke

items.docs: Calls a PostgreSQL function (stored procedure) via a remote procedure call. Function arguments are passed in the request body. Functions must be created in the public schema or an exposed schema.
